observations.summary: 所有 sp-* 工作流技能遵循统一的调用语法：$sp-plan 风格语法，并保留规范的工作流状态标识符
主要功能开发工作流：sp-specify -> sp-plan -> sp-tasks -> sp-implement，支持分支路径 sp-clarify、sp-deep-research、sp-debug
项目认知系统采用三层架构：sp-map-scan（证据收集）-> sp-map-build（图重建）-> sp-map-update（增量更新）
所有工作流技能都要求 spec-kit 项目结构，兼容性检查为 'Requires spec-kit project structure with .specify/ directory'
工作流技能支持多种执行模式：subagent-mandatory、subagents-first、adaptive、subagent-assisted，并包含 Codex 原生子代理集成
工作流技能集成了项目认知运行时，支持 lexicon 和 query 命令进行语义搜索和上下文感知
被动学习层集成：所有工作流技能都支持通过 uvx 命令启动学习、捕获和晋升，维护 .specify/memory/learnings/ 目录
工作流状态管理：使用 workflow-state.md 作为阶段状态真理源，支持 compaction 后恢复
高级后果分析门控：所有工作流技能都包含 Senior Consequence Analysis Gate，用于生命周期、运行状态、破坏性行为、共享状态、下游消费者影响分析
内联项目认知更新：所有工作流技能都遵循共享的内联关闭合同，在更改项目相关文件或行为后运行内联项目认知更新

Rapid fork-to-product evolution: Project started as a source code import from a leaked archive on 2026-03-31 and within 2 months accumulated 843 commits, transforming into an actively developed desktop AI assistant application with Tauri-based desktop shell, goal system, agent teams, plugin architecture, and Spec Kit Plus integration.

Single-primary-author project: 程序员阿江(Relakkes) authored ~87% of all commits. This is a solo-maintainer project with occasional community PRs. The contributor uses multiple name variants (Relakkes Yang, 程序员阿江-Relakkes), likely across different machines or accounts. This concentration creates both velocity and bus-factor risk.

Sequential numbered feature branches (006, 007, 008) indicate a disciplined feature-workflow approach where each major initiative gets its own branch. Current branch '008-specify-discussions-settings' focuses on Spec Kit Plus discussion and settings features. This pattern suggests the project uses a lightweight variant of trunk-based development with numbered feature lanes.

Four distinct development phases visible in commit history: (1) Source import and documentation (late Mar 2026), (2) Core feature development - model support, commands, docs (early Apr 2026), (3) Desktop app and advanced features - Tauri shell, goal system, agent teams, plugins (Apr-May 2026), (4) Spec Kit Plus integration - .specify/ scaffolding, project cognition, skill system, workflow authoring (late May-Jun 2026). Each phase built upon the previous, showing organic architectural growth.

Upstream sync pattern: The project is a fork that periodically syncs with upstream (cc-haha repository). Merge commits like 'Merge upstream/main: 同步上游最新代码' and 'Merge branch main of https://github.com/chenziyang110/cc-haha' show active upstream tracking. This means the project inherits upstream improvements while adding local customizations.

Development velocity is exceptionally high: 838 commits in April-May 2026 combined (avg ~14 commits/day). This suggests rapid prototyping and iterative development. The commit message quality evolved from vague Chinese descriptions to more structured conventional commits over time, indicating growing development discipline.
Commit convention: Conventional Commits (feat:, fix:, docs:, refactor:, test:, chore:, perf:, ci:). Branch naming: fix/xxx, feat/xxx, docs/xxx - no codex/ prefix allowed.

Dual AI instruction system: AGENTS.md for general repository guidelines + CLAUDE.md for Claude-specific workflow rules including Spec Kit Plus managed blocks, sp-* workflow routing, project cognition always-on context, and durable state management.
Commands follow a consistent pattern: type='local-jsx' with lazy-loaded modules, or type='prompt' with inline prompt generation. All satisfy the Command interface.
Tools use buildTool() pattern with Zod input/output schemas, permission checking, and UI rendering functions (renderToolUseMessage, renderToolResultMessage).

Multiple files are auto-generated stubs (proxy-based no-ops) for ant-internal feature-gated modules that are DCE'd in external builds. These include: insights.ts, proactive.ts, torch.ts, subscribe-pr.ts, force-snip.ts, and many tool directories.

The src/commands/ directory contains 90+ slash command subdirectories plus 18 top-level .ts files. The src/tools/ directory contains 59 tool subdirectories plus 1 utils.ts file and a shared/ directory.
Security is pervasive: BashTool has sandbox/permission integration, PowerShellTool has AST-based security analysis, file tools check write permissions, web tools check pre-approved hosts.
The CLI has three entrypoint patterns: cli.tsx (bootstrap with fast-paths), init.ts (memoized initialization), mcp.ts (MCP server). Dynamic imports are used extensively to minimize startup time.
